coaching.coaching: Pin down acceptance criteria for documentation updates with a concrete worked example.
coaching.subject: acceptance criteria for documentation updates

coaching.coaching: State the concrete attestation evidence the [operator-attested] post-extraction architecture documentation obligation will require (a change id / vote outcome / timestamp), recorded on the ticket.
coaching.subject: post-extraction architecture documentation obligation

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC that verifies _config_sources.py has no import of `rebar.config`, e.g.: `! grep -rE 'from rebar\.config|import rebar\.config' src/rebar/_config_sources.py`
evidence: Plan text: 'The leaf imports ONLY stdlib + rebar._config_schema + rebar._deprecations (no import of rebar.config), so it is a true leaf with no back-edge.'
ABSENCE: No AC contains an import-graph check, a grep for 'from rebar.config import' inside _config_sources.py, or a tox/pytest step that would fail on a circular import.
findings.finding: The plan body explicitly promises that _config_sources.py 'imports ONLY stdlib + rebar._config_schema + rebar._deprecations (no import of rebar.config)' — a structural invariant that prevents a circular-import regression — but no Acceptance Criterion verifies this constraint. Behavioral tests (AC #7) cannot catch a stray import that doesn't break test logic.
findings.location: 'New seam' section (leaf import constraint) + Acceptance Criteria (no matching AC)
scenarios: An implementer accidentally imports a config.py symbol (e.g. a helper used transitively) into _config_sources.py; all behavioral tests pass because the circular import resolves at runtime in CPython, but the 'true leaf' invariant is silently broken and future callers get import-time errors in certain load orders.
findings.suggested_fix:
Insert after AC #1:
- [ ] `_config_sources.py` does not import `rebar.config`; verify: `! grep -E 'from rebar\.config|import rebar\.config' src/rebar/_config_sources.py`
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a persistent record of the three-layer config architecture (e.g., a brief prose paragraph in docs/architecture.md under a 'Config subsystem' heading, or a module-level docstring in _config_sources.py describing its role as the raw-input leaf) so the layering rationale survives beyond this ticket.
evidence: Plan removes arch.md offenders row but has no AC or step that adds a prose description of the new three-module structure anywhere.
The plan's own prose ('raw-input source layer', 'sits strictly BELOW the typed-merge/cache/precedence machinery') is the only structured explanation — and it lives only in the ephemeral ticket.
No mention of updating docs/architecture.md beyond deleting the offenders row, no ADR, no AGENTS.md update, no module docstring requirement in any AC.
findings.finding: The plan deletes the stale config.py offenders row from docs/architecture.md but does not add any persistent documentation (arch.md update, ADR, AGENTS.md, or module-level docstring) describing the resulting three-layer config architecture (_config_schema → _config_sources → config.py). A future developer landing on the config subsystem will find three private modules with no documented rationale for the split.
findings.location: Plan sections 'arch.md remedy row is STALE' and 'New seam'; AC item 5 (`! grep -q "^| .config.py. |" docs/architecture.md`)
scenarios: A developer adding a new config feature needs to understand which layer to put it in — with no persistent doc of the three-layer model, they may re-open config.py or create a fourth module inconsistently.
A code reviewer sees _config_sources.py and _config_schema.py alongside config.py with no explanation of their relationship in any checked-in document.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a section to docs/architecture.md (or update an existing 'Config subsystem' entry) with one short paragraph: '`config.py` is the public resolver/cache layer; `_config_sources.py` is the raw-input leaf (filesystem discovery, TOML parsing, env overrides); `_config_schema.py` holds the typed dataclass/schema. Each layer imports only from layers below it.' Alternatively, add equivalent docstrings at the top of each `_config_*.py` module.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a 'was split' documentation paragraph to `docs/architecture.md` (following the established pattern at lines 279–363) describing the `_config_sources.py` extraction: what moved, what stayed in `config.py`, and how the re-export back-compat and monkeypatch seam are preserved.
evidence: docs/architecture.md lines 279–363 show every prior completed split (src/rebar/__init__.py, applier.py, outbound_differ.py, _cli/__init__.py, _engine_support/reads.py) has a dedicated 'was split' paragraph documenting what moved, what stayed, and how back-compat is preserved

The plan enumerates AC5 ('The stale config.py remedy row is gone') but does not enumerate adding the 'was split' prose entry that the docs/architecture.md section consistently carries for completed splits
ABSENCE: no mention of adding documentation prose to docs/architecture.md under '### Current offenders' or a new paragraph analogous to the existing completed-split paragraphs
findings.finding: docs/architecture.md has an established pattern of adding a 'was split' prose paragraph whenever a completed extraction graduates a file off the allowlist (see the __init__.py, applier.py, outbound_differ.py, _cli/__init__.py, _engine_support/reads.py paragraphs at lines 279–363). The plan deletes the stale offenders-table row for config.py (AC5) but does not enumerate adding such a documentation paragraph describing the _config_sources.py extraction — an unenumerated edit target against the module's own documentation convention.
findings.location: docs/architecture.md (lines 279–363 pattern) / plan section 'Back-compat' and AC5
scenarios: An implementor executing the plan AC-by-AC removes the offenders row but leaves no documentation of what _config_sources.py contains or the seam rationale, violating the established architecture.md documentation convention
A future agent reading architecture.md finds no record of the _config_sources.py extraction, making it harder to understand the config module's decomposition history
findings.suggested_fix: After the offenders table, add a paragraph following the existing pattern, e.g.: '`src/rebar/config.py` was **split** along the raw-input source seam: `repo_root`, `config_file`, `tracker_dir_override`, `_TOML_CACHE`, `_parse_toml`, `_read_toml_table`, `_pyproject_rebar_state`, `_discover_project_config`, `user_config_path`, `_CANONICAL_ENV_NAMES`, `_canonical_env_name`, `_LEGACY_ENV_ALIASES`, `_map_legacy_env`, `env_overrides`, and `_strict_unknown_keys` moved to the new leaf `src/rebar/_config_sources.py`. `config.py` re-exports every moved name (`from rebar._config_sources import X as X`) so `rebar.config.<name>` attribute access and `from rebar.config import <name>` are unchanged. The typed-merge/cache machinery (`load_config`, `_ordered_layers`, `_resolve`, `_RESULT_CACHE`) stays in `config.py`. This brought `config.py` back under the soft cap (dropped from the allowlist).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Harden AC5's grep to match the literal backtick-quoted cell text, e.g. `! grep -qF '| `config.py` |' docs/architecture.md`, to eliminate basic-regex wildcard false-positives.
evidence: VERIFY-COMMAND DEFECT TAXONOMY type (1): dots in basic-regex grep are wildcards, not literal characters, creating a substring false-positive risk.
findings.finding: The grep pattern in AC5 (`^| .config.py. |`) uses basic-regex `.` wildcards rather than literal dots, so it could match unintended table rows (e.g. `| _config_pyX |`) and produce a false failure, violating the 'substring false-positive' proving-command rule.
scenarios: A future architecture.md table row whose text incidentally matches the wildcard pattern causes the AC to fail even though the stale row is correctly removed.
A CI run on a slightly different Markdown table format unexpectedly passes even if the row was not removed, because a similar row satisfies the negated grep.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `! grep -q "^| .config.py. |" docs/architecture.md` with `! grep -qF '| `config.py` |' docs/architecture.md` (using `-F` for fixed-string matching against the literal Markdown table cell content).

findings.finding: The ticket has no `## Acceptance Criteria` checklist. A plan cannot be reviewed for completion without testable, checkable criteria.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an `## Acceptance Criteria` section with `- [ ]` checklist items, one per observable, in-session-verifiable outcome.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
